core_case.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: mild Binance UI-versus-API implementation ambiguity despite otherwise clear venue/time/close rules
core_case.freshness_driver: Binance BTC/USDT price path and minute-level volatility into the Apr. 16 12:00 ET settlement window
decision_blockers: Freshness risk: a one-day crypto contract can reprice materially before settlement
Exact-minute Binance wick/path dependence is still not independently quantified
No stronger volatility-based verification beyond spot/candle checks was added at synthesis stage

structured_handoff_primary.contract_ambiguity_reason: mild Binance UI-versus-API implementation ambiguity despite otherwise clear venue/time/close rules
independently_verified_points: Binance BTC/USDT remains the governing settlement venue and pair
Resolution is the Apr. 16 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute candle close, equivalent to 16:00 UTC
Current Binance spot is around 74.03k, leaving roughly a 2.0k cushion above 72k
Recent Binance 24h low around 73.5k remained above the strike during the verification pass
Sidecar summaries were broadly faithful to the raw persona findings
structured_handoff_primary.verification_gap_summary: The main unresolved gap is how much downside volatility or wick risk arrives in the final pre-settlement hours.
structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: BTC is already >$2k above the strike and recent Binance lows stayed above 72k, so the market’s high-Yes pricing may simply be correct.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: Different weighting of exact-minute path risk versus the current cushion above 72k.
structured_handoff_primary.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle at Apr. 16 12:00 ET closes strictly above 72,000.

verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker should use the deterministic selected-input bundle as its verification boundary; additional agent-side tool use is disallowed in this mode.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Market's high-Yes view is broadly justified by Binance BTC/USDT trading around 74.1k, but the exact noon ET 1-minute close condition makes 87.5% slightly too aggressive; estimate 84% Yes.
main_logical_chain: Start from the live 87.5% market prior and verify the exact contract mechanics
Check Binance, the settlement venue, and confirm BTC is currently trading materially above 72k
Perform an extra contextual verification pass with CoinGecko and recent Binance history
Conclude the market is directionally right but trim modestly for path dependence in a narrow time-window contract
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot and recent candles were around 74.1k during the run
Polymarket market price and order book centered near 87.5% Yes
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance 1-minute close at 12:00 ET, so normal BTC volatility could still flip the result
A ~2.1k cushion is meaningful but not huge on a one-day Bitcoin horizon
unresolved_ambiguities: How much normal BTC intraday volatility should discount a high current spot cushion in this exact-minute format
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A move back toward or below 73k, a material negative catalyst before noon ET, or Binance-specific weakness would make the view more bearish; sustained stability above 74k into settlement would make it more bullish.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Highly timing-sensitive: the contract resolves on the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle close at 12:00 ET on 2026-04-16, verified as 2026-04-16T16:00:00Z in metadata.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High quality on contract mechanics and direct venue pricing via Polymarket/Gamma and Binance; medium evidence independence because CoinGecko is only a contextual cross-check within the same broader market ecosystem.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is currently above 72k on Binance, so Yes is favored, but the market is too confident for a one-minute tomorrow settlement and timing risk is underpriced.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1m close the governing source of truth.
Direct Binance data shows BTC currently above 72,000 by a meaningful but not enormous margin.
strongest_supports: Binance spot was around 74,034 at review time, roughly 2,034 above strike.
strongest_disconfirmers: The market resolves on one exact Binance 1m close tomorrow, not on broad daily price conditions.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much intraday volatility arrives before 2026-04-16 16:00 UTC.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move toward market if a later verification closer to resolution still showed a comfortable cushion with subdued volatility; I would move lower if BTC compresses toward 73k or shows unstable minute-level action before noon ET.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: The contract resolves on the Binance BTC/USDT candle closing at 2026-04-16 12:00 ET, verified as 16:00 UTC, so path and exact-minute timing matter materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality direct Binance settlement-relevant data plus Polymarket rules for contract interpretation; source-of-truth ambiguity is low but evidence independence is only medium.
